images.caption: Figure 1:Loss landscapes between Pretraining and downstream SFT. While pretraining exhibits monotonic improvement, SFT reveals a loss basin, indicating that scaling either model size or token count beyond a critical threshold leads to performance degradation.
Figure 3:Structural correspondence between Shannon communication model[1948BSTJ...27..379S]and LLMs.
Figure 4:Evolution of Pythia loss contours under varying Gaussian noise levels. The emergence of a loss basin at higher noise levels.
Figure 5:Pythia loss evolution under varying SFT learning rates on GSM8K. The emergence of a loss basin at higher noise levels.
Figure 6:Evolution of Pythia loss contours under varying quantization bit-widths. The emergence of a loss basin at higher noise levels.

Figure 7:Fitting performance comparison: Shannon Law (Solid) vs. QiD Baseline (Dashed). The plots illustrate loss trajectories across varying Gaussian noise levels (10–40 dB) for fixed token budgets. While both laws align in high-SNR regimes (bottom curves), our Shannon Law demonstrates superior robustness in noise-dominated settings (top curves, e.g., 10 dB), tightly tracking the U-shaped degradation where the QiD baseline systematically deviates.

Figure 8:3D Loss Landscapes: ours (orange surface) vs. QiD law (blue wireframe). Our method demonstrates superior geometric flexibility, maintaining high fidelity (R2=0.9555R^{2}=0.9555) even in the extreme 10 dB regime where the baseline (R2=0.8251R^{2}=0.8251) fails to track the steep curvature of the data manifold.

Figure 9:Evolution of Pythia loss contours on GSM8K under SFT with varying learning rates (8 values). As the learning rate increases, corresponding to stronger optimization noise, the loss landscape transitions from a smooth monotonic regime to a noise-dominated basin. In this regime, increasing model size (NN) or training tokens (DD) fails to improve performance, illustrating the breakdown of conventional scaling under extreme perturbations.

Figure 10:Evolution of Pythia loss contours on SiQA under SFT with varying learning rates (8 values). As the learning rate increases, corresponding to stronger optimization noise, the loss landscape transitions from a smooth monotonic regime to a noise-dominated basin. In this regime, increasing model size (NN) or training tokens (DD) fails to improve performance, illustrating the breakdown of conventional scaling under extreme perturbations.

Figure 11:Evolution of Pythia loss contours on StarCoder under SFT with varying learning rates (8 values). As the learning rate increases, corresponding to stronger optimization noise, the loss landscape transitions from a smooth monotonic regime to a noise-dominated basin. In this regime, increasing model size (NN) or training tokens (DD) fails to improve performance, illustrating the breakdown of conventional scaling under extreme perturbations.
